claims.text: The copilot-app target is hidden from auto-detection and explicit --target copilot-app installs fail until the copilot-app experimental flag is enabled.
Workflows are stored in a SQLite database at ~/.copilot/data.db.
A .prompt.md belongs to exactly one surface, determined by whether it carries workflow frontmatter (interval, schedule_hour, schedule_day) or not.
Only interval, schedule_hour, and schedule_day at the top level mark a .prompt.md as a workflow shape.
apm install inserts workflow rows with enabled = 0 (always disabled on install).
When a workflow is already installed and execution-affecting fields change, apm install resets enabled = 0 and clears next_run_at.
apm uninstall deletes only APM-namespaced rows (apm--<owner>--<pkg>--<prompt>); user-authored rows are never touched.
APM calls the Copilot App's create_project_from_path over IPC when the App is running and reachable on its loopback WebSocket.
When the Copilot App is closed, APM falls back to direct SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE resolver selecting by main_repo_path (UNIQUE).
Workflow rows are locked in the lockfile using the copilot-app-db://workflows/<namespaced-id> URI scheme.
APM guards database writes with PRAGMA user_version and refuses to write below version 13.
APM issues BEGIN IMMEDIATE with bounded retry to coexist with the Copilot App's writer connection in WAL mode.
The database resolution order is APM_COPILOT_APP_DB first, then ~/.copilot/data.db.
apm install --global with workflow-shape prompts emits a one-time warn-and-proceed diagnostic about CWD being ~/.copilot instead of a project.
APM intentionally does NOT accept mode: autopilot via the copilot-app target as a secure-by-default behaviour until package signing ships.
